key_insight: A detailed finite element model was used to iteratively refine the WST Altitude Structure, balancing stiffness, mass, stress, and dynamic performance for the telescope’s large steel support frame.
engineering_relevance: The study provides design criteria for a full-scale telescope structure where structural weight, deformation, and resonance control directly affect pointing accuracy and operational reliability.
method: Built an FE model of the steel altitude structure with detailed beam and plate dimensions.
Applied representative boundary conditions to simulate operational loading and support conditions.
Used static and modal results to guide cross-section and thickness optimization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation of structural weight, mechanical deformations, stresses, and frequency response, including local and global resonance modes, to compare preliminary and refined designs.
watch_item: Because the model relies on representative boundary conditions and preliminary-to-refined assumptions, final performance should be checked against detailed load cases and validation data.
paper_title: WST, the Wide-field Spectroscopic Telescope: Telescope structure FE analyses
